principles.label: Failure is a Process of Elimination
principles.summary: Failure is not a defeat, but a necessary process of discovering methods that do not work, bringing you closer to the correct solution.
principles.details: Every unsuccessful attempt eliminates a wrong path. Success often requires pushing through the urge to quit, as people frequently give up without realizing they are on the brink of achieving their goal.
principles.representative_quote: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.

principles.label: Innovate Entire Systems, Not Just Components
principles.summary: A successful invention requires the simultaneous development of a complete, integrated supporting ecosystem.
principles.details: Creating a single isolated product (like a lightbulb) is insufficient. For an invention to be commercially viable and widely adopted, the entire infrastructure required to support it (generators, distribution cables, meters, switches) must also be built.
principles.representative_quote: instead of just fabricating pieces of the electric puzzle, it was Edison’s intention to create a whole system, from electrical generation and distribution to the end products for home and business use.

principles.label: Inventions Must Be Practical and Profitable
principles.summary: Technical solutions must be driven by commercial economy and actual market demand.
principles.details: An invention cannot succeed if it is too expensive to implement at scale. Pursuing only practical and profitable inventions sustains the high costs of research and ensures the technology actually reaches and benefits society.
principles.representative_quote: His method of raising money was to pursue only the inventions that were both 'practical and profitable.'

principles.label: Success Requires Continuous Hard Work
principles.summary: True success and the capture of opportunity demand relentless effort and a willingness to pay the price of hard labor.
principles.details: Many fail because they demand shorter hours and more pay without putting in the necessary effort. Opportunities are frequently missed because they are disguised as hard work.
principles.representative_quote: Any boy can be a success, providing he is willing to pay the price, which is continuous hard work.

principles.label: Prove Ideas Before Patenting
principles.summary: Reduce an idea to actual practice and ensure it works before applying for a patent.
principles.details: Ideas are easy to generate, but developing them into a commercial shape is a long, tedious, and expensive process. Obtaining patents for ideas that cannot be commercially exploited is a waste of resources.
principles.representative_quote: I suggest that if the young inventor has an idea he had better reduce it to actual practise and be sure that it works before applying for a patent.

principles.label: Continuous Improvement
principles.summary: No matter how wonderful the current record of achievement, it can always be improved.
principles.details: Massive success should not be viewed as a final endpoint, but as a foundation that future generations of engineers and innovators will continue to build upon.
principles.representative_quote: the record is wonderful but fine as it is it can be made still better and I have a feeling that in your hands it will be

principles.label: Nature as Supreme Intelligence
principles.summary: What people call God is actually Nature, a supreme intelligence that rules matter, rather than a merciful or loving personal deity.
principles.details: Observation of the natural world shows it is not inherently kind or merciful, suggesting that a supreme intelligence governs matter without human-like emotions.
principles.representative_quote: what you call God I call Nature, the Supreme intelligence that rules matter.

principles.label: Credit-Based Monetary Reform
principles.summary: Governments should issue credit-based money rather than debt-based money.
principles.details: It is absurd for taxpayers to be compelled to pay double the principal in interest when the government has the power to produce money as a credit instead.
principles.representative_quote: if the government can produce debt-based money, it could equally as well produce money that was a credit to the taxpayer.

principles.label: Honor Shared Sacrifices
principles.summary: We must always remember and honor the crucial role our allies played in our shared victories.
principles.details: Despite pride in national achievements, victory in global conflicts is impossible without the fierce and overwhelming sacrifices made by allied nations.
principles.representative_quote: let us remember always that the war could not have been won if the belgians the British the French and the Italians had not fought like Bulldogs in the face of overwhelming odds

principles.label: Intellectual Humility
principles.summary: Reserve judgment on concepts you do not comprehend.
principles.details: It is better to refuse to offer an opinion on complex theories if you do not understand the underlying science.
principles.representative_quote: I don't think anything of Einstein theory because I can't understand it

frameworks.label: The Industrial Invention Factory
frameworks.summary: A systematic approach to producing marketable products more productively than a lone inventor could achieve.
frameworks.details: Steps: 1) Start with a definite idea of a practical result. 2) Collect all possible scientific and commercial data. 3) Assign dedicated teams of researchers to work on specific projects. 4) Systematically test materials and iterate until a viable product is found. 5) Develop the surrounding systems required to commercialize the product.
frameworks.representative_quote: I always start out with a definite idea of accomplishing a certain result. I collect all the data possible... I then proceed to sketch out every possible and probable way of attaining results and carry it to success by experimenting.

frameworks.label: Analogous System Design
frameworks.summary: Using a prevailing, widely adopted system as a structural and economic benchmark to guide the development of a new, disruptive technology.
frameworks.details: By designing a new system to mimic the familiar parameters of an existing one (e.g., designing electric lights to permit subdivision analogous to gas jets), you ensure the new technology will be familiar to users and commercially competitive.
frameworks.representative_quote: before starting experiments on the incandescent electric light system many memorandums were drawn up as to a general system that would permit of the subdivision of the lights into small units analgous to gas Jet

mental_models.label: Whole System Design
mental_models.summary: Approaching a new technology not as an isolated device, but as an entire ecosystem that includes generation, distribution, and end-use products.
mental_models.details: Instead of just inventing the light bulb, Edison founded the illuminating company, built the power station, and laid electrical conduits to deliver the necessary power.
mental_models.representative_quote: instead of just fabricating pieces of the electric puzzle, it was Edison’s intention to create a whole system

mental_models.label: Disappointments as the 'Salt of the Inventor'
mental_models.summary: Viewing failures, rejections, or the discovery that an idea is already patented not as a defeat, but as a necessary test of character that ultimately leads to triumph.
mental_models.details: When an inventor faces setbacks, they must use the disappointment to fuel their resilience rather than as a reason to quit.
mental_models.representative_quote: But disappointments show the salt of the inventor. Only by such disappointments can he triumph finally.

mental_models.label: Opportunity as Labor
mental_models.summary: The understanding that high-value opportunities rarely present themselves as easy wins, but rather as demanding, unglamorous work.
mental_models.details: Most people overlook their best chances for success because they are unwilling to engage in the tedious, difficult labor required to realize them.
mental_models.representative_quote: Opportunity is missed by most people because it is dressed in overalls and looks like hard work.

mental_models.label: The Human as an Aggregate
mental_models.summary: Viewing a human being not as a singular, indivisible entity or soul, but as a collective aggregate of smaller living components (cells).
mental_models.details: Edison compared a human being to New York City, illustrating the absurdity of an aggregate entity having a singular afterlife.
mental_models.representative_quote: I am not an individual—I am an aggregate of cells, as, for instance, New York City is an aggregate of individuals. Will New York City go to heaven?

heuristics.label: Always try just one more time
heuristics.summary: When faced with failure, the most certain way to succeed is to make one additional attempt.
heuristics.representative_quote: Our greatest weakness lies in giving up. The most certain way to succeed is always to try just one more time.

heuristics.label: Only pursue practical and profitable inventions
heuristics.summary: Filter potential projects by their ability to generate commercial demand and sustain themselves economically.
heuristics.representative_quote: His method of raising money was to pursue only the inventions that were both 'practical and profitable.'

heuristics.label: First plan—then act
heuristics.summary: Collect all possible scientific and commercial data and sketch out every probable path before beginning physical experimentation.
heuristics.representative_quote: In other words, first plan—then act.

heuristics.label: Don't patent without demand
heuristics.summary: Avoid patenting anything for which there is no actual market demand, as it wastes time, money, and energy.
heuristics.representative_quote: I have made a rule in my later years, not to patent anything for which I knew there was no actual demand.

heuristics.label: Design for commercial economy
heuristics.summary: Ensure technical solutions are superior to prevailing alternatives in terms of cost and resource efficiency.
heuristics.representative_quote: this necessitated the creation of lamps of very high resistance to diminish the enormous investment in Copper necessary

signature_quotes.label: 10,000 ways that won't work
signature_quotes.summary: Edison's famous reframe of failure as a process of elimination.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.

signature_quotes.label: Opportunity dressed in overalls
signature_quotes.summary: Edison's observation on why people miss opportunities.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Opportunity is missed by most people because it is dressed in overalls and looks like hard work.

signature_quotes.label: Our greatest weakness
signature_quotes.summary: Edison's advice on persistence and trying one more time.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Our greatest weakness lies in giving up. The most certain way to succeed is always to try just one more time.

signature_quotes.label: How close they were to success
signature_quotes.summary: Edison's reflection on people who quit too soon.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Many of life's failures are people who did not realize how close they were to success when they gave up.

signature_quotes.label: Ideas are easy
signature_quotes.summary: Edison's reality check on the difficulty of commercializing an idea.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Ideas are easy, but working them into commercial shape is generally a long, tedious, and expensive job.

signature_quotes.label: God is Nature
signature_quotes.summary: Edison's definition of the supreme intelligence.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: what you call God I call Nature, the Supreme intelligence that rules matter.

signature_quotes.label: Continuous hard work
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Any boy can be a success, providing he is willing to pay the price, which is continuous hard work.

anti_patterns.label: Giving up in the face of failure
anti_patterns.summary: Quitting when faced with obstacles or repeated failures.
anti_patterns.details: You might be incredibly close to success without realizing it, making the act of quitting the actual cause of failure. Giving up is the greatest weakness.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: Many of life's failures are people who did not realize how close they were to success when they gave up.

anti_patterns.label: Ignoring the supporting infrastructure
anti_patterns.summary: Focusing solely on the core invention while neglecting the systems required to deliver it.
anti_patterns.details: Without the necessary accessories (meters, switches, efficient dynamos, and distribution systems), the core invention cannot be delivered to customers or measured for commercial billing.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: a host of accessories like underground CS switches sockets Etc were necessary to make a complete system of distribution

anti_patterns.label: Acting as a 'pure scientist'
anti_patterns.summary: Focusing solely on theoretical knowledge instead of practical, marketable results.
anti_patterns.details: It fails to produce practical, marketable results that can be commercialized to fund further research and benefit society.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: his systematic approach to inventing focused on practical results rather than theoretical knowledge.

anti_patterns.label: Patenting unproven or unwanted ideas
anti_patterns.summary: Collecting patents for inventions that have no market demand or haven't been reduced to practice.
anti_patterns.details: It leads to wasted money and effort because turning a raw idea into a commercial product is tedious and expensive, and protecting an idea that cannot be commercially exploited is useless.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: Merely collecting patents is a waste of time, money, and energy.

anti_patterns.label: Expecting glamorous opportunities
anti_patterns.summary: Demanding shorter hours and more pay, or expecting opportunities to be easy.
anti_patterns.details: Opportunities are often disguised as hard labor ('dressed in overalls'), causing people who avoid hard work to overlook them and become a drag on their industry.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: Opportunity is missed by most people because it is dressed in overalls and looks like hard work.
